contents.title: Grime Geometry Binding - v1
contents.description: Per-region grime and wear geometry binding for characters, vehicles, and world surfaces, structurally aligned with GameNext BCI and visual binding contracts.

id.description: Stable binding identifier, e.g., character-torso-grime, character-legs-grime, vehicle-body-grime.

region.description: Logical region identifier such as head, torso, legs, hands, boots, vehicle-body, vehicle-wheels, environment-tile.

gates.description: Invariant-style gates reused from GameNext contracts, enabling safety and quality control for grime intensity and update rates.

CIC.description: Constellation Integrity Coefficient; used to gate overall system confidence and disable aggressive grime updates when low.

AOS.description: Attention or orientation score; can bias where grime is emphasized (e.g., focus areas).

DET.description: Detection reliability; used to downscale grime if exposure sensing or contact events are unreliable.

LSG.description: Latency Safety Guard; high values should dampen fast, extreme changes in grime to protect performance and clarity.

UEC.description: Optional entertainment/engagement metric; may influence stylization intensity for grime.

visual.description: Normalized visual parameters describing grime, wetness, and wear for this region.

wetness.description: Surface wetness; drives darkening, specular highlights, and drip effects.

mud.description: Mud or thick dirt accumulation; drives albedo overlays and height/normal variation.

soot.description: Soot, smoke, or burn mark intensity; often mapped to dark, soft overlays.

dust.description: Fine dust or sand accumulation; used for subtle roughness and color shifts.

scratchIntensity.description: Overall strength of scratches and scuffs; applied via detail normal and albedo maps.

tearIntensity.description: Cloth tears or material damage; can drive opacity masks and frayed edges.

flowDirX.description: X component of normalized flow direction (e.g., gravity-driven streaking for rain or mud).

flowDirY.description: Y component of normalized flow direction.

stretchDirX.description: X component of normalized stretch direction for anisotropic wear (e.g., cloth or rubber).

stretchDirY.description: Y component of normalized stretch direction.

paletteHex.description: Optional RGB hex palette for grime colors; normalized to a fixed-size array (e.g., 5 entries) before reaching shaders, using the same normalization strategy as GameNext palettes.

surface.description: Static or slowly-varying material properties that shape how grime appears.

baseRoughness.description: Underlying material roughness before grime, aligned with PBR workflows.

baseMetallic.description: Base metallic factor; used to modulate how grime affects highlights.

porosity.description: How easily liquid grime is absorbed; higher values reduce long-lived wet streaks.

fiberDensity.description: Relative density of fibers for fabrics; affects how dust and mud are distributed.

grimeAdhesion.description: General coefficient for how strongly grime adheres to this surface.

audio.description: Optional audio feedback parameters for grime-related state changes.

clothRustleGain.description: Fabric movement audio intensity; can increase with dust and tearIntensity.

squelchGain.description: Wet/mud squelch sound intensity; typically scaled by wetness and mud.

frictionLevel.description: Surface friction audio modulation; influenced by roughness and grime load.

meta.description: Optional metadata for analytics, routing, and engine integration.

materialTag.description: Engine-side tag for material, e.g., cloth, leather, metal, paint, rubber.

environmentTag.description: Environment classification, e.g., desert, marsh, snow, city, jungle.

style.description: Style pack identifier when grime is part of a style (e.g., Urban-Grime, Desert-Dust).
